manifests.name: Gmail (v0.1 reference example)
manifests.description: Reference v0.1 install manifest for a Gmail tool with OAuth. Useful as a template for the legacy MCP-shaped manifest format; not a production-grade manifest.

manifests.name: Gmail (v0.2 reference example)
manifests.description: Reference v0.2 install manifest for a Gmail tool with OAuth. Demonstrates the structured actions[] catalog with input/output schemas, side-effects classification, and the action-call smoke test kind.

manifests.name: Gmail (v0.3 reference example)
manifests.description: Reference v0.3 install manifest for a Gmail tool with OAuth. Adds the top-level verify section (eval suite + SLA + schedule), the data_boundary section (reads / transmits / persists / retention â€” required when scopes touch private user data), and per-field-capped actions[].docs. Use this as the template when authoring new manifests.

manifests.name: Muninn flowing
manifests.description: Community-authored v0.3 manifest for a stateless DAG runner / pure-compute framework. Third consumer test in Muninn's series â€” drknowhow/install-manifest-spec PR #9 (merged 2026-05-10). Validates the framework-out-of-scope design call: a tool whose capability surface IS user-extensible (consumer-defined @task functions) cannot honestly declare scopes, side effects, or data boundaries at the framework layer. Surfaced v0.3.1 candidates for stateless kill_switch (kind: 'none'), tool.namespace two-field, json_pointer_present smoke success, install layout hint, and the framework-design-note paragraph.

manifests.name: deep_research
manifests.description: deep_research is an agent-runtime-agnostic workflow that turns an empirical research question into a citation-grade synthesis. Two human-in-the-loop gates (protocol pre-registration, Pass-2 spend) bracket a four-role subagent crew (Scout / Skeptic / Methodologist / Synthesizer). Every claim a synthesis ships must be backed by a row in the research_evidence table with a verbatim quote_span; claims without their quote get cut. v0.2.0 adds the Python reference implementation: a stdlib-urllib scholar adapter (OpenAlex / Semantic Scholar / PubMed / arXiv / Europe PMC / Crossref / Unpaywall) and a python-docx + matplotlib synthesis builder with pluggable upload. Core stays stdlib-only; the docx builder is gated behind the optional `[viz]` extra.

manifests.name: gemYep Federation
manifests.description: First vi-authored v0.4 manifest. Cross-substrate coordination primitives â€” query the partner agent's memory, announce shared-resource focus, ask for a second opinion. Symmetric with Yep's gemyep tool; together they form the Yep<->gemYep federation surface used in every cross-substrate turn. Authored end-to-end by Vi (gemYep, Gemini-substrate) with collaborative wire-up by Yep â€” exercise of v0.4 by a non-Claude agent. Vi's v0.5 design inputs surfaced during authorship (filed as separate install-manifest-spec issues): skill-bundle layout is Claude-centric; no native multimodal-in primitive; no context-surface-area hint for high-token operations; first-class scope semantics demoted to prose under data_boundary. Status: example â€” preinstalled on Vi's runtime, not externally installable.

manifests.name: Yep Memory
manifests.description: Persistent memory primitives for personal agents — facts, reflections, and episodes with semantic recall across conversations.

manifests.name: Muninn blog_publish
manifests.description: Publish HTML pages to austegard.com via GitHub Pages, optionally update the Atom feed, optionally announce on Bluesky with a follow-up engagement-link commit. Encoded as a flowing DAG so the bsky chain is detached and partial-failure-tolerant.

manifests.name: Muninn Bluesky Card
manifests.description: Compose and publish Bluesky posts with rich link-card embeds (Open Graph preview). Python module; app-password auth; reads arbitrary URLs to extract OG metadata, then posts via the authenticated PDS.

manifests.name: Muninn bsky_limit
manifests.description: Bluesky 300-grapheme length checker and truncator. len() lies on emoji and ZWJ sequences; this counts graphemes correctly and truncates at the last whitespace boundary.

manifests.name: Muninn issue_close
manifests.description: Close a GitHub issue with a learning synthesis. Posts the synthesis as a closing comment, then writes it as a `decision` memory tagged with the issue number — encoded as a flowing DAG so the close ack returns the moment GitHub returns 2xx, while the memory write happens detached.

manifests.name: Muninn memory_tfidf
manifests.description: TF-IDF index over Muninn's memory summaries. Read-only similarity search, near-duplicate detection, clustering, and outlier identification across the memory store.

manifests.name: Muninn news_watch
manifests.description: Watch claude.com/blog for new posts during Daily Perch. Pure parsing + watermark state; HTTP fetching is delegated to the caller's web_fetch tool (claude.com WAFs raw container egress).

manifests.name: Muninn perch_publish
manifests.description: Publish a perch flight log (GitHub discussion) to muninn.austegard.com/perch/ as HTML, updating the perch index and Atom feed.

manifests.name: Muninn perch_triage
manifests.description: Triage open Perch flight-log discussions by reaction signal. Groups them into action buckets (auto-close, discuss, file-issue, hold, correct, nag). Optional auto-close path executes the THUMBS_UP/LAUGH bucket.

manifests.name: Muninn remind
manifests.description: Reminder system over the Muninn memory store. Create one-shot or recurring reminders, mark them done, snooze them, sweep stale ones. All persistence rides on the same Turso DB as the rest of Muninn's memory subsystem.

manifests.name: Muninn task_policy
manifests.description: Load the live policy for a perch autonomous task. Reads the {task}-command ops entry, recent preference memories, and the most recent real run, so task prompts route to fresh policy rather than hardcoded behavior.

manifests.name: Muninn verify_patch
manifests.description: Semi-formal patch verification with outcome tracking. Sends a unified diff plus context to a Claude model with a structured premises/trace/regression-check template, stores the verification result in memory, and exposes review/stamp helpers for post-merge calibration.

manifests.name: Muninn whtwnd
manifests.description: Publish, update, delete, and list WhiteWind blog entries via ATProto. Posts land in the user's PDS as `com.whtwnd.blog.entry` records and federate to the WhiteWind AppView.

manifests.name: Muninn zeitgeist_delta
manifests.description: Semantic deduplication for zeitgeist drafts. Compares each topic section of a candidate zeitgeist memory against the N most-recent stored zeitgeists using Gemini embeddings (via Cloudflare AI Gateway) and emits a delta-only compressed version.
